content.problemStatement:
As usual, Tom is trying to catch Jerry. To accomplish this task, Tom hired KKK other cats (Tom does not take part in the chase). Formally, they are in a graph GGG with NNN vertices and MMM edges. Let's denote the set of vertices of GGG by VVV. The graph has a special property: for each simple cycle containing at least four vertices, there is an edge which is not part of this cycle, but connects two distinct vertices on the cycle.

Jerry can only move along the edges of the graph, but arbitrarily fast. The cats don't have to follow the edges ‚ÄĒ they can use a helicopter to go to any vertices they want.

The process of catching Jerry happens in several rounds as follows:


In round 000, the cats choose a set of vertices X0‚äÜVX_0 \subseteq VX0‚Äč‚äÜV with ‚ą£X0‚ą£‚Č§K|X_0| \le K‚ą£X0‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§K; Jerry chooses a vertex v0‚ąąV‚ąĖX0v_0 \in V \setminus X_0v0‚Äč‚ąąV‚ąĖX0‚Äč.
For each i‚Č•0i \ge 0i‚Č•0, let's denote the set of vertices containing cats in round iii by XiX_iXi‚Äč and the vertex containing Jerry by viv_ivi‚Äč. In round i+1i+1i+1, the cats choose a subset Xi+1‚äÜVX_{i+1} \subseteq VXi+1‚Äč‚äÜV with ‚ą£Xi+1‚ą£‚Č§K|X_{i+1}| \le K‚ą£Xi+1‚Äč‚ą£‚Č§K.
Afterwards in round i+1i+1i+1, Jerry must choose a vertex vi+1‚ąąV‚ąĖXi+1v_{i+1} \in V \setminus X_{i+1}vi+1‚Äč‚ąąV‚ąĖXi+1‚Äč such that there is a path from viv_ivi‚Äč to vi+1v_{i+1}vi+1‚Äč which doesn't pass through any vertex in Xi‚ą©Xi+1X_i \cap X_{i+1}Xi‚Äč‚ą©Xi+1‚Äč. If Jerry cannot choose such a vertex, the cats win.
If this process can last forever (KKK cats are not enough to catch Jerry), then Jerry wins.

Your task is to calculate the minimum number of cats KKK which Tom has to call in order to guarantee that Jerry will be caught (there is no winning strategy for Jerry).
constraints: there is at most one edge between each pair of vertices
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains two space-separated integers NNN and MMM.
MMM lines follow. Each of these lines contains two space-separated integers uuu and vvv denoting an edge between vertices uuu and vvv.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer ‚ÄĒ the minimum number of cats needed to catch Jerry.
